highlights.text: I wrote on Friday at 4pm that for Monday: “Bear case Monday: Begins under 5720.” On Monday morning at 8am, we lost this structure, and we got a selloff of corresponding magnitude: Down 160+ points.
Readers know though that after an elevator down sell like yesterday, a short squeeze is common, and it typically occurs on my core setup: The failed Breakdown.
I wrote yestreday at 4pm: “If we head down to new lows tomorrow, the Failed Breakdown (my Failed Breakdown) of today’s low is obviously of interest.

While ES looked good to start the day, it was again sideswiped by Tariff remarks, causing a flush to new lows in the 5530s, then putting in the exact same Failed Breakdown discussed above at 2pm for a second time.
I call this a “double dip Failed Breakdown”.

post_title: SPX Tries A Bounce But Can It Stick? CPI Tomorrow Decides. March 12 Plan
